subject: Sports
title: Boca Juniors vs River Plate
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Boca Juniors vs River Plate.

hook.text: The 'Superclasico' is the most intense derby on Earth. Buenos Aires splits in half for every match.

buildup.text: Both clubs were founded in the La Boca neighborhood. River moved uptown; Boca stayed working class.

discovery.text: Boca became the club of the people. River became the club of wealth. Class warfare played out on the pitch.

twist.text: In the 2018 Copa Libertadores final, River fans attacked the Boca bus. The second leg was moved to Madrid.

climax.text: Despite the violence, the Superclasico remains football's rawest expression of passion and tribal identity.

punchline.text: Two clubs, one neighborhood, an entire nation divided. Football at its wildest.

quiz.question: What class divide defines the Boca vs River Plate rivalry?
options: Boca represents the working class; River represents wealth
Boca is from the north; River from the south
One club plays in a higher league than the other
